title: Core Models Path
description: Build fluency in Core Models inside Neural Networks by moving from foundational terms into adjacent methods, variants, and extensions.

steps.title: Deep Belief Network (DBN)
steps.summary: Deep Belief Network (DBN) belongs to the Neural Networks area of AI/ML, within Core Models. It sits inside Neural Networks / Core Models. Start after Deep Belief Network and Deep Q-Network. It opens into Deep Belief Networks (DBN) Extensions and Deep Belief Networks (DBN) Extensions Extensions.
steps.whyIncluded: Start here to anchor the vocabulary and core intuition for this area.

steps.title: Neural Turing Machines (NTMs)
steps.summary: Neural Turing Machines (NTMs) belongs to the Neural Networks area of AI/ML, within Core Models. It sits inside Neural Networks / Core Models. Start after Neural Network and Neural Networks. It opens into Neural Turing Machines (NTMs) Variants and Neural Turing Machines (NTMs) Techniques.
steps.whyIncluded: Start here to anchor the vocabulary and core intuition for this area.

steps.title: Graph Convolutional Network (GCN)
steps.summary: Graph Convolutional Network (GCN) belongs to the Neural Networks area of AI/ML, within Core Models. It sits inside Neural Networks / Core Models. Start after Graph Convolutional Network and Graph. It opens into graph convolutional network (GCN) embeddings and Temporal Graph Convolutional Network (T-GCN).
steps.whyIncluded: Use this after the foundations to connect patterns, variants, and system behavior.

steps.title: Small-world Network
steps.summary: Small-world Network belongs to the Neural Networks area of AI/ML, within Core Models. It sits inside Neural Networks / Core Models. Start after World Model and World Models. It opens into Kleinberg Small-World Model and hierarchical navigable small world (HNSW).

steps.title: Deep Neural Network
steps.summary: Deep Neural Network belongs to the Neural Networks area of AI/ML, within Core Models. It sits inside Neural Networks / Core Models. Start after Deep Q-Network and Neural Network. It opens into Deep Graph Neural Networks and D2NN (Diffractive Deep Neural Networks).
steps.whyIncluded: Treat this as an advanced extension once the earlier terms feel natural.
